title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: outlines propose change rasterpic. detailed discussion contributing package, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("dieghernan/rasterpic", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: contributing guide adapted tidyverse contributing guide available https://tidyverse.tidyverse.org/CONTRIBUTING.html.

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: direct option use base plot() methods provided terra package, terra::plotRGB(): Figure 1: Plot terra package
code: library(rasterpic) library(terra)  # Use the flag of the United Kingdom. img <- system.file("img/UK_flag.png",   package = "rasterpic" )  uk <- sf::st_read(   system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg",     package = "rasterpic"   ),   quiet = TRUE )  uk_img <- rasterpic_img(uk, img, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE) plotRGB(uk_img)

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: tidyterra package provides ggplot2 support terra SpatRaster objects: Figure 2: Plot tidyterra package

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: tmap package can also create maps SpatRaster objects: Figure 3: Plot tmap package

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: mapsf package can also plot SpatRaster objects: Figure 4: Plot mapsf package
code: library(mapsf)  mf_raster(uk_img) mf_scale()  mf_inset_on(x = "worldmap", pos = "topright") mf_worldmap(uk) mf_inset_off()

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: maptiles package can download map tiles different providers. also provides functions plotting terra SpatRaster objects: Figure 5: Plot maptiles package
code: library(maptiles)  other_tile <- get_tiles(uk, crop = TRUE, zoom = 6)  other_tile_crop <- terra::crop(other_tile, uk_img)  plot_tiles(other_tile_crop) plot_tiles(uk_img, add = TRUE)

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: Tennekes M (2018). “tmap: Thematic Maps R.” Journal Statistical Software, 84(6), 1–39. doi:10.18637/jss.v084.i06. Giraud T (2026). mapsf: Thematic Cartography. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.mapsf. Hernangómez D (2023). “Using tidyverse terra objects: tidyterra package.” Journal Open Source Software, 8(91), 5751. ISSN 2475-9066. doi:10.21105/joss.05751. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.05751. Hijmans R, Brown , Barbosa M (2026). terra: Spatial Data Analysis. R package version 1.9-34, https://rspatial.org/. Wickham H (2016). ggplot2: Elegant Graphics Data Analysis. Springer-Verlag New York. ISBN 978-3-319-24277-4. https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org.

title: Get started
text: example geotags image using shape Austria: Figure 1: Image geotagged coordinates Austria.
code: library(sf) library(terra) library(rasterpic)  # Load plotting packages. library(tidyterra) library(ggplot2)  # Set the spatial object and image. x <- read_sf(system.file("gpkg/austria.gpkg", package = "rasterpic")) img <- system.file("img/vertical.png", package = "rasterpic")  # Geotag the image. default <- rasterpic_img(x, img)  autoplot(default) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: rasterpic_img() provides options expansion, alignment, cropping masking. expand argument expands raster extent beyond spatial object: Figure 2: Example image expansion. halign valign arguments control alignment image within spatial extent: Figure 3: Example image alignment. crop, mask inverse arguments control whether raster cropped object extent masked object shape: Figure 4: Example masked image. Figure 5: Example inverse masked image.
code: expand <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 1)  autoplot(expand) +   geom_sf(data = x) bottom <- rasterpic_img(x, img, valign = 0)  autoplot(bottom) +   geom_sf(data = x) mask <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(mask)  maskinverse <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(maskinverse)

title: Get started
text: expand argument expands raster extent beyond spatial object: Figure 2: Example image expansion.
code: expand <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 1)  autoplot(expand) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: halign valign arguments control alignment image within spatial extent: Figure 3: Example image alignment.
code: bottom <- rasterpic_img(x, img, valign = 0)  autoplot(bottom) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: crop, mask inverse arguments control whether raster cropped object extent masked object shape: Figure 4: Example masked image. Figure 5: Example inverse masked image.
code: mask <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(mask)  maskinverse <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(maskinverse)

title: Get started
text: rasterpic_img() supports following spatial input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars class: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). rasterpic_img() S3 generic. Methods extent-like inputs use object extent, vector methods can also mask image object shape.

title: Get started
text: rasterpic can read following image formats: png files. jpeg/jpg files. tiff/tif files.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Diego Hernangómez. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hernangómez D (2026). rasterpic: Convert Digital Images Spatially Referenced SpatRaster Objects. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.rasterpic. https://dieghernan.github.io/rasterpic/.
code: @Manual{R-rasterpic,   title = {{rasterpic}: Convert Digital Images to Spatially Referenced {SpatRaster} Objects},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.rasterpic},   author = {Diego Hernangómez},   year = {2026},   version = {0.5.1},   url = {https://dieghernan.github.io/rasterpic/},   abstract = {Convert digital images to spatially referenced SpatRaster objects, as defined by the terra package, using coordinates from supported spatial input classes. Supported inputs include numeric coordinate vectors and objects from the sf, terra and stars packages. The main function is an S3 generic, allowing other packages to extend support to additional spatial classes.}, }

what: rasterpic | Convert digital images into geotagged SpatRaster objects
title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into geotagged SpatRaster objects
text: rasterpic tiny package single goal: geotag image return terra SpatRaster object using coordinates supported spatial input classes (see ?terra::SpatRaster). package stable maintained best-effort basis. currently prioritize CRAN compatibility, bug fixes regressions new features.

title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into geotagged SpatRaster objects
text: Install rasterpic CRAN:

title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into geotagged SpatRaster objects
text: rasterpic_img() can geotag image several spatial input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars class: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). rasterpic_img() S3 generic. Methods extent-like inputs use object extent, vector methods can also mask image object shape. example uses sf object:  can also adjust expansion, alignment, cropping masking options:
code: library(rasterpic) library(sf) library(terra)  # Use the flag of the United Kingdom. img <- system.file("img/UK_flag.png", package = "rasterpic") uk <- read_sf(system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg", package = "rasterpic"))  class(uk) #> [1] "sf"         "tbl_df"     "tbl"        "data.frame"  # Geotag the image. uk_flag <- rasterpic_img(uk, img)  uk_flag #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 400, 800, 3  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 5398.319, 5398.319  (x, y) #> extent      : -2542183, 1776472, 6430573, 8589900  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator (EPSG:3857) #> source(s)   : memory #> colors rgb  : 1, 2, 3 #> names       :   r,   g,   b #> min values  :   0,  14,  35 #> max values  : 255, 255, 255  # Plot with ggplot2 and tidyterra. library(tidyterra) library(ggplot2)  autoplot(uk_flag) +   geom_sf(data = uk, color = alpha("blue", 0.5)) # Align, crop and mask the image. uk_flag2 <- rasterpic_img(uk, img, halign = 0.2, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(uk_flag2) +   geom_sf(data = uk, fill = NA)

title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into geotagged SpatRaster objects
text: rasterpic can read following image formats: png files. jpeg/jpg files. tiff/tif files.

Supported inputs include numeric coordinate vectors and objects from the sf, terra and stars packages. The main function is an S3 generic, allowing other packages to extend support to additional spatial classes.}, }

what: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: Compute aspect ratio width divided height, columns divided rows.

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: x SpatRaster object, sf sfc object numeric vector length 4 coordinates c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), created sf::st_bbox().

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: numeric scalar giving aspect ratio.

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
code: # \donttest{ library(terra) #> terra 1.9.34  x <- rast(system.file("tiff/elev.tiff", package = "rasterpic")) plot(x)  asp_ratio(x) #> [1] 2.34375 # }

what: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images to Spatially Referenced 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
title: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images to Spatially Referenced 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
text: Convert digital images spatially referenced 'SpatRaster' objects, defined 'terra' package, using coordinates supported spatial input classes. Supported inputs include numeric coordinate vectors objects 'sf', 'terra' 'stars' packages. main function S3 generic, allowing packages extend support additional spatial classes.

title: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images to Spatially Referenced 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
text: Maintainer: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: Geotag image return SpatRaster based coordinates supported spatial input class. rasterpic_img() S3 generic. See S3 methods supported input classes.

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
code: rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'sf' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'sfc' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'sfg' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'stars' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'bbox' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'numeric' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'SpatVector' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'SpatExtent' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: x R object. See S3 methods supported classes. img image geotag. can local file URL, example "https://.imgur.com/6yHmlwT.jpeg". Accepted file extensions png, jpg, jpeg, tif tiff. halign number 0 1 giving horizontal alignment img relative x. 0 aligns img left edge x, 1 aligns right edge 0.5 centers horizontally. valign number 0 1 giving vertical alignment img relative x. 0 aligns img bottom edge x, 1 aligns top edge 0.5 centers vertically. expand expansion factor bounding box x. 0 means expansion added, 1 means bounding box expanded double original size. See Details. crop Logical. raster cropped (expanded) bounding box x? See Details. ... arguments passed methods. mask Logical, vector methods. raster masked shape x? See Details. inverse Logical. used mask = TRUE. TRUE, areas raster covered x masked. crs Character string describing CRS. parameter applies x SpatExtent, sfg, bbox numeric coordinate vector. See CRS section.

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: SpatRaster object (see terra::rast()) layer corresponds color channel img: img least 3 layers, result records layers 1 3 red, green blue channels names "r", "g" "b" alpha applicable. img already RGB specification (may case tif/tiff files), result keeps specification. resulting SpatRaster RGB specification explained terra::RGB().

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: vignette("rasterpic", package = "rasterpic") explains effect parameters halign, valign, expand, crop mask examples.

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: rasterpic supports spatial input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars class: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). packages can provide methods additional spatial classes. Methods extent-like inputs use object extent. Methods vector inputs can also mask image object shape.

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: function preserves CRS x applicable. optimal results, use geographic coordinates (longitude/latitude). crs can WKT format, "authority:number" code "EPSG:4326" PROJ-string "+proj=utm +zone=12". can also retrieved : sf::st_crs(25830)$wkt. terra::crs(). tidyterra::pull_crs(). See Value Notes sections terra::crs().

title: Geotag an image as a SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
code: # \donttest{ library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(terra) library(ggplot2) library(tidyterra) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘tidyterra’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter  x_path <- system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg", package = "rasterpic") x <- st_read(x_path, quiet = TRUE) img <- system.file("img/vertical.png", package = "rasterpic")  # Use the default configuration. ex1 <- rasterpic_img(x, img)  ex1 #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 333, 250, 3  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 6484.467, 6484.467  (x, y) #> extent      : -1193414, 427703.2, 6430573, 8589900  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator (EPSG:3857) #> source(s)   : memory #> colors rgb  : 1, 2, 3 #> names       :   r,   g,   b #> min values  :  15,   8,   4 #> max values  : 254, 255, 254  autoplot(ex1) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5)   # Expand the bounding box. ex2 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 0.5)  autoplot(ex2) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5)   # Align the image to the left edge. ex3 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, halign = 0)  autoplot(ex3) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Align")   # Crop to the bounding box. ex4 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE)  autoplot(ex4) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Crop")   # Mask to the vector shape. ex5 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(ex5) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Mask")   # Mask outside the vector shape. ex6 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(ex6) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Mask Inverse")   # Combine cropping and inverse masking. ex7 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(ex7) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Combine")  # }

title: rasterpic 0.5.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-23 User-facing errors, warnings notes now use cli alerts inline markup consistent argument, file URL formatting. rasterpic_img() mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE returns SpatRaster RGB specification (terra::.RGB() returns TRUE).

title: rasterpic 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-21 Package documentation reviewed updated AI-assisted editing. rasterpic_img() now S3 generic methods supported spatial input classes (#39). rasterpic_img() now supports stars objects.

title: rasterpic 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-21 Migrated vignettes Quarto.

title: rasterpic 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-14 New logo. minimum required R version now 4.1.0. Minor internal documentation improvements.

title: rasterpic 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-03-19 output 3 layers, first three renamed "r", "g" "b". cases 4 layers , fourth layer renamed "alpha". allows compatibility tmap >= 4.0 avoids error ! [subset] select layer name unique.

title: rasterpic 0.2.6
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-17 Updated documentation.

title: rasterpic 0.2.5
text: CRAN release: 2024-04-12 Updated documentation. Made Title Description fields DESCRIPTION specific.

title: rasterpic 0.2.4
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-18 Ensured SpatVector objects always CRS. image fewer 3 layers, raster output RGB specification (terra::.RGB() FALSE). expected corner case. image file (e.g., tiff) already RGB specification, preserve output. Updated documentation tests.

title: rasterpic 0.2.3
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-08 Made asp_ratio() internal function. Improved documentation tests.

title: rasterpic 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-18 Removed unused dependencies. Improved documentation. Fixed typos messages. Declared output RGB raster terra::RGB(). Added ggplot2 “Suggests”.

title: rasterpic 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-06-10 Added tidyterra “Suggests”.

title: rasterpic 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-18 terra: SpatVector, SpatExtent. sf: sfg, sf::st_bbox(). Numeric vectors c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). img parameter rasterpic_img() now accepts image URLs. Added article pkgdown site. Fixed CRAN errors.

title: rasterpic 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-01-27 Initial release.
